examples.title: DFA Minimization using Partition Refinement
examples.description: Algorithm to minimize a DFA by iteratively refining partitions of states
examples.explanation: The algorithm starts by separating accepting from non-accepting states, then iteratively splits groups where states have different transition behavior to distinguishable states. When no more splits occur, equivalent states remain in the same group.

examples.title: Table-Filling Method for Distinguishable States
examples.description: Algorithm to find all distinguishable state pairs using table-filling approach
examples.code:
// Input: DFA (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F)
// Output: Set of distinguishable state pairs

BEGIN
 n ← |Q|
 states ← array of Q elements
 table ← n×n boolean matrix, initialized to FALSE
 
 // Step 1: Mark initial distinguishable pairs
 FOR each pair (p, q) where p ∈ F and q ∉ F DO
 table[p][q] ← TRUE
 table[q][p] ← TRUE
 END FOR
 
 // Step 2: Iteratively mark more pairs
 changed ← TRUE
 WHILE changed DO
 changed ← FALSE
 FOR each unmarked pair (p, q) DO
 FOR each input symbol a IN Σ DO
 r ← δ(p, a)
 s ← δ(q, a)
 IF table[r][s] = TRUE OR table[s][r] = TRUE THEN
 table[p][q] ← TRUE
 table[q][p] ← TRUE
 changed ← TRUE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 END WHILE
 
 // Step 3: Unmarked pairs are equivalent
 RETURN all pairs where table[p][q] = FALSE
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm marks pairs where one state is accepting and other is non-accepting as initially distinguishable, then repeatedly checks if transitions from an unmarked pair lead to a marked (distinguishable) pair. When stable, unmarked pairs are equivalent.

examples.title: Python Implementation of DFA Minimization
examples.description: Complete working example of DFA minimization
examples.explanation: This Python implementation shows practical DFA minimization including unreachable state removal, completion with dead state, and partition refinement. The final partitions represent equivalence classes that become states in the minimal DFA.
